title: Japanese Character Counter & Manuscript Converter
description: Count Japanese text characters with genkou yoshi (400-char manuscript paper) conversion, X/Twitter weighted count, and resume length guide. Free online tool.
placeholder: Type or paste your text here...
genkouNote: Equivalent number of 400-character manuscript paper pages.
twitterNote: Full-width/CJK characters count as 2, half-width characters count as 1 (X/Twitter weighted count).
resumeNote: Approximate character count guidelines. Newlines are excluded. For reference only.

howToUse.label: Enter your text
howToUse.description: Type or paste text into the textarea. All counts update in real time.

howToUse.label: Check manuscript pages and Twitter count
howToUse.description: See the equivalent number of 400-char manuscript pages and the X/Twitter weighted character count (full-width=2, half-width=1) with progress bars.

howToUse.label: Compare with resume guidelines
howToUse.description: Recommended character ranges for common resume sections are shown as color bars. Green = within range, yellow = below minimum, red = over.

faq.question: How is the manuscript page count calculated?
faq.answer: The total character count (including newlines) is divided by 400. For example, 1200 characters = 3.0 pages of 400-character manuscript paper.

faq.question: How does the Twitter/X character count work?
faq.answer: X/Twitter counts CJK (Japanese, Chinese, Korean) and full-width characters as 2 units each. The 280-unit limit is equivalent to 140 full-width Japanese characters.

faq.question: What is the difference between half-width and full-width characters?
faq.answer: Half-width characters include ASCII (letters, numbers, symbols) and half-width katakana. Full-width characters include hiragana, katakana, kanji, and full-width alphanumerics.

faq.question: How many characters should a cover letter be?
faq.answer: In Japanese job applications, motivation letters and self-PR sections are typically 100–300 characters. Check the job posting for specific requirements. The guidelines shown are for reference only.

faq.question: How is this different from the regular character counter?
faq.answer: The regular character counter shows total character count. This tool specializes in Japan-specific use cases: manuscript paper conversion, Twitter weighted count, and resume guidelines.
